paragraphs: Currently, the UPI ecosystem facilitates seamless digital payments through a standardized message protocol, enabling interoperability among various banks and payment service providers. However, there is a growing demand for enhanced security measures, particularly in biometric-authenticated transactions, to prevent unauthorized access and ensure user trust. To address this gap, the issuer has decided to mandate the implementation of a nonce-based challenge mechanism for all biometric transactions, designated by refCategory='05', to strengthen cryptographic replay-attack prevention. This directive aims to enhance the security framework of UPI transactions, ensuring compliance with evolving security standards and user expectations.
NPCI has decided to enhance the UPI transaction flow by integrating biometric authentication as a mandatory feature for all high-value transactions.
The technical artifacts affected by this decision include the REQ_PAY and RESP_PAY message types, which will now require additional biometric data fields.
The Common Library (CL) must be updated to support the new clVersion indicating biometric capability, and the CRED Block will incorporate encrypted biometric credentials.
The REQ_AUTH_DETAILS and RESP_AUTH_DETAILS APIs will be modified to handle the exchange of biometric authentication data between the PSP and the NPCI Switch.
This section covers Participant Impact & Obligations.
Please disseminate the information contained herein to the officials concerned.
Product managers must ensure that all relevant teams are briefed on the new feature specifications and integration requirements.
Engineers are advised to review the technical documentation and update any system components affected by the new feature implementation.
Compliance teams must verify that the feature aligns with current regulatory standards and update any compliance documentation as necessary.
